---
title: "HBV Series: Model Introduction"
subtitle: "Understanding the HBV QSP Model and Treatment Mechanisms"
author:
  - Vijay Ivaturi
---

:::{.callout-note}
## HBV Tutorial Series

This is **Tutorial 1 of 6** in the HBV series.

| # | Tutorial | Status |
|---|----------|--------|
| 1 | **Model Introduction (current)** | ← You are here |
| 2 | [Copula Vpop Generation](hbv_02_copula_vpop_tutorial.qmd) | |
| 3 | [Global Sensitivity Analysis](hbv_03_gsa_tutorial.qmd) | |
| 4 | [Structural Identifiability](hbv_04_structural_identifiability_tutorial.qmd) | |
| 5 | [ILP Calibration](hbv_05_ilp_calibration_tutorial.qmd) | |
| 6 | [VCT Simulation](hbv_06_vct_simulation_tutorial.qmd) | |

:::

## Learning Outcomes

Hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection is a major global health challenge,
with over 290 million people chronically infected.
Mathematical models help us understand treatment mechanisms and predict clinical outcomes.

In this tutorial, you will learn:

- The biology of HBV infection and why it's difficult to cure
- The structure of the 11-ODE QSP model capturing HBV dynamics
- The treatment mechanisms of NUC (nucleoside analogs) and IFN (interferon)
- How to define and simulate the HBV model in Pumas
- Key clinical endpoints: Functional Cure and HBsAg Loss

:::{.callout-tip}
## Prerequisites

This tutorial assumes basic familiarity with:

- Pumas model syntax (`@param`, `@dynamics`, `@derived`)
- Ordinary differential equations (ODEs)
- Pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modeling concepts

If you've completed the [Tumor Burden Tutorial Series](tb_01_model_introduction_tutorial.qmd),
you have all the background needed.
:::

## Background

### The HBV Infection Challenge

**Hepatitis B Virus (HBV)** infects liver cells (hepatocytes)
and establishes chronic infection in many patients.
Unlike hepatitis C, which can now be cured with antivirals, HBV is much harder to eliminate because:

1. **cccDNA persistence**:
   The virus inserts its genetic material (cccDNA) into the liver,
   creating a reservoir that survives treatment
2. **Immune exhaustion**: Chronic infection leads to T-cell exhaustion,
   reducing the body's ability to clear infected cells
3. **HBsAg shedding**: Infected cells produce large amounts of surface antigen
   (HBsAg) that may "decoy" the immune system

### Treatment Goals

Current treatments aim for different levels of control:

| Endpoint | Definition | Clinical Meaning |
|:---------|:-----------|:-----------------|
| **Viral Suppression** | HBV DNA < 25 copies/mL | Virus under control (but not eliminated) |
| **HBsAg Loss** | HBsAg < 0.05 IU/mL | Surface antigen cleared |
| **Functional Cure** | HBsAg Loss + Viral Suppression for ≥24 weeks off-treatment | Durable control without ongoing treatment |

:::{.callout-important}
## Why Functional Cure Matters

Current treatments (NUC therapy) can suppress virus indefinitely,
but patients must take medication for life.
**Functional cure** means patients can stop treatment without viral rebound—
the "holy grail" of HBV therapy.
:::

### Treatment Modalities

**Nucleoside/Nucleotide Analogs (NUCs):**

- Examples: Entecavir, Tenofovir
- Mechanism: Block viral replication by inhibiting reverse transcriptase
- Effect: Rapidly suppress HBV DNA, slowly reduce HBsAg
- Limitation: Rarely achieve functional cure alone

**Interferon (IFN):**

- Examples: Pegylated interferon-alpha (PEG-IFN)
- Mechanism: Enhances immune response, promotes infected cell killing
- Effect: Boosts T-cell activity, accelerates HBsAg decline
- Limitation: Side effects limit duration, variable response

## Libraries

```{julia}
#| output: false
using Pumas
using DataFramesMeta
using CategoricalArrays
using AlgebraOfGraphics
using CairoMakie
using Random
```

## Part 1: Model Structure Overview

### The 11-ODE System

The HBV QSP model tracks 11 state variables organized into three compartments:

```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
flowchart LR
    subgraph Hepatocytes ["Hepatocyte Populations"]
        T["T<br/>Target cells"]
        I["I<br/>Infected cells"]
        R["R<br/>Resistant cells"]
    end

    subgraph Viral ["Viral Dynamics"]
        V["V<br/>Virus (HBV DNA)"]
        S["S<br/>HBsAg"]
    end

    subgraph Immune ["Immune Response"]
        E["E<br/>Effector T-cells"]
        D["D<br/>Dendritic cells"]
        Q["Q<br/>Delayed signal"]
        X["X<br/>Cytotoxic effect"]
        Y["Y<br/>Dead cell marker"]
        Z["Z<br/>ALT proxy"]
    end

    T -->|"infection<br/>β×T×V"| I
    I -->|"virus production<br/>p_V"| V
    I -->|"HBsAg production<br/>p_S"| S
    I -->|"activates"| D
    D -->|"stimulates"| E
    E -->|"kills"| I
    I -->|"converts to"| R
    X -->|"promotes<br/>resistance"| R

    style T fill:#ccffcc,stroke:#00cc00
    style I fill:#ffcccc,stroke:#cc0000
    style R fill:#ffffcc,stroke:#cccc00
    style V fill:#cce5ff,stroke:#0066cc
    style S fill:#cce5ff,stroke:#0066cc
    style E fill:#ffccff,stroke:#cc00cc
```

### State Variables Explained

| State | Symbol | Description | Units |
|:------|:-------|:------------|:------|
| Target hepatocytes | T | Uninfected liver cells | cells |
| Infected hepatocytes | I | Cells producing virus | cells |
| Resistant hepatocytes | R | Cells resistant to cytotoxic effect | cells |
| Virus | V | HBV DNA | copies/mL |
| Surface antigen | S | HBsAg molecules | molecules |
| Effector T-cells | E | Immune cells that kill infected cells | cells |
| Dendritic cells | D | Antigen-presenting cells | cells |
| Delayed signal | Q | Immune activation delay | - |
| Cytotoxic effect | X | Cumulative killing signal | - |
| Dead cell marker | Y | Products from cell death | - |
| ALT proxy | Z | Liver inflammation marker | - |

### Parameter Structure

The model has **22 fixed parameters** (from literature/prior studies)
and **9 estimated parameters** (with inter-individual variability):

**Estimated Parameters (with IIV):**

| Parameter | Description | Typical Value (log10) |
|:----------|:------------|:----------------------|
| β (beta) | Infection rate | -5.0 |
| p_S | HBsAg production rate | 8.0 |
| m | Immune killing rate | 2.5 |
| k_Z | ALT production rate | -5.0 |
| convE | Effector conversion | 2.5 |
| epsNUC | NUC efficacy | -2.0 |
| epsIFN | IFN efficacy | -1.5 |
| r_E_IFN | IFN immune boost | 0.3 |
| k_D | DC activation | -4.0 |

:::{.callout-note}
## Log10-Scale Parameterization

Parameters are on the **log10 scale** with additive random effects.
This achieves log-normal behavior when used in dynamics as `10^param`:

```text
Individual value = 10^(typical_value + η)
```

This is equivalent to multiplicative IIV on the natural scale.
:::

## Part 2: The Pumas Model Definition

Let's build the HBV model.

### The Full Model

```{julia}
hbv_model = @model begin
    @param begin
        # Fixed parameters
        iniV ∈ RealDomain()
        p_V ∈ RealDomain()
        r_T ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        r_E ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        T_max ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        n ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        phiE ∈ RealDomain()
        dE_X ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        phiQ ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        d_V ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        d_TI ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        d_E ∈ RealDomain()
        d_Z ∈ RealDomain()
        d_Q ∈ RealDomain()
        rho ∈ RealDomain()
        r_X ∈ RealDomain()
        d_X ∈ RealDomain()
        d_Y ∈ RealDomain()
        Smax ∈ RealDomain()
        phiS ∈ RealDomain()
        nS ∈ RealDomain()
        d_D ∈ RealDomain()
        iniZ ∈ RealDomain()

        # Estimated parameters (with IIV) - log10 scale
        tvbeta ∈ RealDomain()
        tvp_S ∈ RealDomain()
        tvm ∈ RealDomain()
        tvk_Z ∈ RealDomain()
        tvconvE ∈ RealDomain()
        tvepsNUC ∈ RealDomain()
        tvepsIFN ∈ RealDomain()
        tvr_E_IFN ∈ RealDomain()
        tvk_D ∈ RealDomain()

        # Inter-individual variability (9 parameters)
        Ω ∈ PDiagDomain(9)

        # Residual error
        σ_HBsAg ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
        σ_V ∈ RealDomain(lower = 0.0)
    end

    @random begin
        η ~ MvNormal(Ω)
    end

    # Time-varying covariates for treatment phases
    @covariates dNUC dIFN

    @pre begin
        # Individual parameters - additive on log10 scale
        beta = tvbeta + η[1]
        p_S = tvp_S + η[2]
        m = tvm + η[3]
        k_Z = tvk_Z + η[4]
        convE = tvconvE + η[5]
        epsNUC_ind = tvepsNUC + η[6]
        epsIFN_ind = tvepsIFN + η[7]
        r_E_IFN_ind = tvr_E_IFN + η[8]
        k_D = tvk_D + η[9]

        # Treatment effects (depend on dosing covariates)
        eps_NUC = dNUC > 0 ? (dNUC < 1 ? epsNUC_ind / dNUC : epsNUC_ind) : 0.0
        eps_IFN = dIFN > 0 ? (dIFN < 1 ? epsIFN_ind / dIFN : epsIFN_ind) : 0.0
        rr_E_IFN = dIFN > 0 ? (dIFN < 1 ? 10^(r_E_IFN_ind * dIFN) : 10^r_E_IFN_ind) : 1.0

        # Initial conditions
        V_0 = 10^iniV
        I_0 = d_V * V_0 / (10^p_V)
        T_0 = T_max - I_0
        S_0 = 10^p_S * I_0 / d_V
        Z_0 = 10^iniZ
        lambda_Z = (10^d_Z) * Z_0
    end

    @init begin
        T = T_0
        V = V_0
        S = S_0
        Z = Z_0
        I = I_0
    end

    @vars begin
        infection_active = I / (I + 0.0001)
        SAg_ug_per_ml = (V + S) * (96 * 24000 * 1.0e6) / (6.023e23)

        # HBsAg in IU/mL (log10 scale)
        HBsAg_IU = SAg_ug_per_ml / 0.98
        log_HBsAg = log10(max(HBsAg_IU, 0.04))

        # Viral load (log10 copies/mL)
        log_V = log10(max(V, 1.0e-6))
    end

    @dynamics begin
        # Infected hepatocytes
        I' = (10^beta) * T * V + r_T * I * (1 - (T + I + R) / T_max) -
            (10^m) * rr_E_IFN * E * I - (10^rho) * X * I - d_TI * I

        # Target hepatocytes
        T' = -(10^beta) * T * V + r_T * T * (1 - (T + I * infection_active + R) / T_max) -
            d_TI * T + (10^rho) * R / 100 - (10^(m - n)) * rr_E_IFN * E * T

        # Resistant hepatocytes
        R' = (10^rho) * X * I * infection_active + r_T * R * (1 - (T + I * infection_active + R) / T_max) -
            (10^rho) * R / 100 - d_TI * R - (10^(m - n)) * rr_E_IFN * E * R

        # Virus dynamics (NUC and IFN reduce production)
        V' = (10^p_V) * 10^(eps_NUC + eps_IFN) * I * infection_active - d_V * V

        # HBsAg dynamics
        S' = (10^p_S) * I * infection_active - d_V * S

        # Dead cell marker
        Y' = (10^m) * rr_E_IFN * E * I * infection_active +
            (10^(m - n)) * rr_E_IFN * E * (T + R) - d_Y * Y

        # Immune response (ALT proxy)
        Z' = lambda_Z + (10^k_Z) * Y - (10^d_Z) * Z

        # Dendritic cell activation
        D' = (10^k_D) * I / (10^phiE + I) * infection_active - (10^d_D) * D

        # Effector T cells
        E' = (10^d_E + r_E * E) * D - dE_X * E * Q^4 / (phiQ^4 + Q^4) - 10^d_E * E

        # Delayed effector signal
        Q' = 10^d_Q * D - 10^d_Q * Q

        # Cytotoxic effect
        X' = r_X * (1 - X) * (I / (10^phiE + I)) * infection_active *
            (1 - Smax * SAg_ug_per_ml^nS / (phiS^nS + SAg_ug_per_ml^nS)) - d_X * X
    end

    @derived begin
        # Observations with residual error
        HBsAg_obs ~ @. Normal(log_HBsAg, σ_HBsAg)
        V_obs ~ @. Normal(log_V, σ_V)
    end

    @observed begin
        # ALT proxy (log10)
        log_ALT = @. log10(max(Z, 1.0e-6))
    end
end
```

### Fixed Parameters

```{julia}
# Fixed parameter values (not varied between patients)
hbv_fixed_params = (
    iniV = -0.481486,      # Initial viral load (log10)
    p_V = 2.0,             # Viral production rate (log10)
    r_T = 1.0,             # Hepatocyte proliferation rate
    r_E = 0.1,             # Effector cell expansion rate
    T_max = 13600000.0,    # Maximum hepatocytes
    n = 2.0,               # Difference in killing rate
    phiE = 2.0,            # Half-saturation for immune activation (log10)
    dE_X = 0.3,           # E to X conversion rate
    phiQ = 0.8,            # Q saturation constant
    d_V = 0.67,            # Virus clearance rate
    d_TI = 0.0039,         # Hepatocyte death rate
    d_E = -2.0,            # Effector cell death rate (log10)
    d_Z = -0.328,          # Z clearance rate (log10)
    d_Q = -2.414,          # Q clearance rate (log10)
    rho = -3.0,            # Cell resistance conversion (log10)
    r_X = 1.0,             # X growth rate
    d_X = 0.2,             # X death rate
    d_Y = 0.22,            # Y clearance rate
    Smax = 1.0,            # Maximum HBsAg suppression
    phiS = 0.147,          # HBsAg suppression saturation
    nS = 0.486,            # HBsAg suppression Hill coefficient
    d_D = -0.62157,        # D clearance rate (log10)
    iniZ = 1.25,            # Initial Z value (log10)
)

println("Fixed parameters defined: ", length(hbv_fixed_params), " parameters")
```

### Estimated Parameters

Initial values of the estimated parameters:

```{julia}
hbv_estimated_init_params = (
    tvbeta = -5.0,
    tvp_S = 8.0,
    tvm = 2.5,
    tvk_Z = -5.0,
    tvconvE = 2.5,
    tvepsNUC = -2.0,
    tvepsIFN = -1.5,
    tvr_E_IFN = 0.3,
    tvk_D = -4.0,

    Ω = Diagonal(ones(9)),

    σ_HBsAg = 0.1,
    σ_V = 0.1,
)
```

## Part 3: Understanding Treatment Mechanisms

### NUC (Nucleoside Analog) Effect

NUCs block viral production by inhibiting reverse transcriptase:

$$V' = 10^{p_V} \cdot \underbrace{10^{\epsilon_{NUC} + \epsilon_{IFN}}}_{\text{treatment reduction}} \cdot I - d_V \cdot V$$

When `dNUC = 1`:

- `eps_NUC` is active (negative value, e.g., -2.0)
- Viral production drops by factor of $10^{-2} = 0.01$ (100-fold reduction)

### IFN (Interferon) Effect

IFN has dual mechanisms:

1. **Viral suppression** (like NUC):
   - Reduces viral production via `eps_IFN`

2. **Immune enhancement**:
   - Boosts effector T-cell killing via `rr_E_IFN`
   - $\text{killing rate} = m \cdot \underbrace{rr\_E\_IFN}_{\text{IFN boost}} \cdot E \cdot I$

### Treatment Arms

The model supports four treatment scenarios:

| Arm | dNUC | dIFN | Description |
|:----|:-----|:-----|:------------|
| 0 | 0 | 0 | Control (no treatment) |
| 1 | 1 | 0 | NUC only |
| 2 | 0 | 1 | IFN only |
| 3 | 1 | 1 | NUC + IFN combination |

## Part 4: Basic Simulation

Let's simulate a typical patient without treatment to see natural disease progression.

### Create a Subject

```{julia}
# Default population parameters
pop_params = merge(hbv_fixed_params, hbv_estimated_init_params)

# Create a subject with no treatment
subject_no_tx = Subject(
    id = "No Treatment",
    covariates = (dNUC = 0, dIFN = 0),
    time = 0.0:7.0:365.0  # Weekly for 1 year
)
```

### Simulate Natural History

```{julia}
sim_no_tx = simobs(
    hbv_model,
    subject_no_tx,
    pop_params,
    zero_randeffs(hbv_model, subject_no_tx, pop_params);
    obstimes = 0.0:1.0:365.0,
    simulate_error = false
)

sim_df = DataFrame(sim_no_tx)
```

### Visualize Natural History

```{julia}
#| fig-cap: "HBV natural history without treatment. Top: Viral load (HBV DNA). Bottom: HBsAg levels. Both remain elevated without intervention."

# Prepare data in long format for AoG
sim_long = stack(
    sim_df[:, [:time, :log_V, :log_HBsAg]],
    [:log_V, :log_HBsAg],
    variable_name = :Biomarker,
    value_name = :Value
)
sim_long.Biomarker = replace(
    sim_long.Biomarker,
    "log_V" => "HBV DNA (copies/mL)",
    "log_HBsAg" => "HBsAg (IU/mL)"
)

# Create plot with AoG
plt = data(sim_long) *
    mapping(:time => "Time (days)", :Value => "log₁₀ Concentration") *
    mapping(row = :Biomarker, color = :Biomarker) *
    #), color = :Biomarker) *
    visual(Lines, linewidth = 2) +
    data((; Value = [log10(0.05)])) * mapping(:Value => "log₁₀ Concentration") * mapping(row = AlgebraOfGraphics.direct("HBsAg (IU/mL)")) * (visual(HLines; color = :green, linestyle = :dash, label = "FC Threshold")) #+ #visual(Annotations))
#    mapping(:Value => AlgebraOfGraphics.direct(log10(0.05)), ) * visual(HLines)

draw(plt, scales(Color = (; palette = [:red, :blue])); legend = (; show = false))

# Add functional cure threshold to the HBsAg panel (second row)
# In AoG 0.7+, ag is a Matrix{AxisEntries} - get the axis from the second row
#ax_hbsag = ag[2, 1].axis  # Second row, first column
#hlines!(ax_hbsag, [log10(0.05)], color = :green, linestyle = :dash, linewidth = 2)
#text!(ax_hbsag, 300, log10(0.05) + 0.3, text = "FC Threshold", fontsize = 12, color = :green)
```

### Compare Treatment Scenarios

```{julia}
#| fig-cap: "Effect of treatment on HBsAg levels. NUC (blue) gradually reduces HBsAg. IFN (green) provides faster decline through immune enhancement. Combination (purple) achieves the fastest reduction."

# Create subjects with different treatment covariates and collect all results
treatments = [
    ("No Treatment", 0, 0),
    ("NUC Only", 1, 0),
    ("IFN Only", 0, 1),
    ("NUC + IFN", 1, 1),
]

# Build DataFrame with all treatment arms
all_sims = DataFrame()
for (label, dnuc, difn) in treatments
    subj = Subject(
        id = label,
        covariates = (dNUC = dnuc, dIFN = difn),
        observations = (HBsAg_obs = nothing, V_obs = nothing),
        time = 0.0:7.0:365.0
    )

    sim = simobs(
        hbv_model, subj, pop_params,
        zero_randeffs(hbv_model, subj, pop_params);
        obstimes = 0.0:1.0:365.0,
        simulate_error = false
    )

    df = DataFrame(sim)
    df.Treatment = fill(label, nrow(df))
    append!(all_sims, df)
end

# Set treatment order for legend
all_sims.Treatment = categorical(
    all_sims.Treatment,
    levels = ["No Treatment", "NUC Only", "IFN Only", "NUC + IFN"]
)

# Create AoG plot
plt = data(all_sims) *
    mapping(
    :time => "Time (days)", :log_HBsAg => "log₁₀ HBsAg (IU/mL)",
    color = :Treatment
) *
    visual(Lines, linewidth = 2)

fig = Figure(size = (900, 500))
ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], title = "HBsAg Dynamics by Treatment Arm")
ag = draw!(ax, plt, scales(Color = (; palette = [:gray, :blue, :green, :purple])))

# Add functional cure threshold (Makie annotation)
hlines!(ax, [log10(0.05)], color = :black, linestyle = :dash, linewidth = 2)
text!(ax, 10, log10(0.05) + 0.3, text = "FC Threshold", fontsize = 12)

# Add legend outside the plot
legend!(fig[1, 2], ag, framevisible = false)

fig
```

## Part 5: Clinical Endpoints

### Defining Endpoints

```{julia}
# Clinical thresholds
const LOQ_HBsAg = 0.05  # IU/mL
const LOQ_V = 25.0      # copies/mL

"""
    is_functional_cure(log_hbsag, log_v)

Determine if a patient has achieved functional cure.
"""
function is_functional_cure(log_hbsag::Real, log_v::Real)
    return log_hbsag < log10(LOQ_HBsAg) && log_v < log10(LOQ_V)
end

"""
    is_hbsag_loss(log_hbsag)

Determine if a patient has achieved HBsAg loss.
"""
function is_hbsag_loss(log_hbsag::Real)
    return log_hbsag < log10(LOQ_HBsAg)
end

println("Endpoint definitions:")
println("  HBsAg Loss: HBsAg < $(LOQ_HBsAg) IU/mL (log10 < $(round(log10(LOQ_HBsAg), digits = 2)))")
println("  Functional Cure: HBsAg Loss AND HBV DNA < $(LOQ_V) copies/mL")
```

### Treatment Phases

A complete HBV VCT simulates multiple phases:

```{julia}
# Treatment phase durations
const HBV_PHASES = (
    untreated = 5 * 365,       # 5 years - establish steady state
    nuc_background = 4 * 365,  # 4 years - NUC background (suppressed patients)
    treatment = 48 * 7,        # 48 weeks - active treatment
    off_treatment = 24 * 7,     # 24 weeks - follow-up
)

println("HBV VCT Treatment Phases:")
println("  1. Untreated: $(HBV_PHASES.untreated) days ($(HBV_PHASES.untreated ÷ 365) years)")
println("  2. NUC Background: $(HBV_PHASES.nuc_background) days ($(HBV_PHASES.nuc_background ÷ 365) years)")
println("  3. Active Treatment: $(HBV_PHASES.treatment) days ($(HBV_PHASES.treatment ÷ 7) weeks)")
println("  4. Off-Treatment: $(HBV_PHASES.off_treatment) days ($(HBV_PHASES.off_treatment ÷ 7) weeks)")
println("  Total: $(sum(HBV_PHASES)) days")
```

## Part 6: Population Variability Preview

Let's see how parameter variability affects outcomes.

```{julia}
#| fig-cap: "HBsAg dynamics with inter-individual variability. Different patients (colors) show varying treatment responses due to random effects on parameters."

Random.seed!(123)

# Sample a few random patients and collect into a DataFrame
n_patients = 10
using LinearAlgebra: Diagonal
params_with_iiv = merge(pop_params, (Ω = Diagonal(fill(0.3, 9)),))

all_patients = DataFrame()
for i in 1:n_patients
    subj = Subject(
        id = i,
        covariates = (dNUC = 1, dIFN = 1),
        observations = (HBsAg_obs = nothing, V_obs = nothing),
        time = 0.0:7.0:365.0
    )

    sim = simobs(
        hbv_model, subj, params_with_iiv;
        obstimes = 0.0:1.0:365.0,
        simulate_error = false
    )

    df = DataFrame(sim)
    df.Patient = fill(string(i), nrow(df))
    append!(all_patients, df)
end

# Create AoG plot with patient as grouping variable
plt = data(all_patients) *
    mapping(
    :time => "Time (days)", :log_HBsAg => "log₁₀ HBsAg (IU/mL)",
    group = :Patient
) *
    visual(Lines, linewidth = 1.5, color = (:steelblue, 0.5))

fig = Figure(size = (900, 500))
ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], title = "HBsAg Variability (NUC + IFN Treatment)")
draw!(ax, plt)

# Add threshold (Makie annotation)
hlines!(ax, [log10(0.05)], color = :green, linestyle = :dash, linewidth = 2)
text!(ax, 10, log10(0.05) + 0.3, text = "FC Threshold", fontsize = 12, color = :green)

fig
```

## Summary

### Key Concepts

1. **HBV infection is difficult to cure** due to cccDNA persistence
   and immune exhaustion

2. **The 11-ODE QSP model captures:**
   - Hepatocyte populations (T, I, R)
   - Viral dynamics (V, S)
   - Immune response (E, D, Q, X, Y, Z)

3. **Treatment mechanisms:**
   - NUC: Blocks viral production
   - IFN: Enhances immune killing + reduces viral production

4. **Parameters are on log10 scale** with additive random effects

5. **Clinical endpoints:**
   - HBsAg Loss: HBsAg < 0.05 IU/mL
   - Functional Cure: HBsAg Loss + Viral Suppression off-treatment

### Model Complexity Comparison

| Aspect | Tumor Burden | HBV QSP |
|:-------|:-------------|:--------|
| State variables | 2 | 11 |
| Estimated parameters | 3 | 9 |
| Fixed parameters | 0 | 22 |
| Treatment covariates | 1 | 2 |
| Clinical endpoints | Response rate | FC, HBsAg Loss |

### What's Next?

In [Tutorial 2: Copula Vpop Generation](hbv_02_copula_vpop_tutorial.qmd),
we'll generate a virtual population for the HBV model using the copula sampling approach,
adapting the techniques from the Tumor Burden series.

### References

::: {#refs}
:::
